root_causes.cause: R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is hard-set to 100 Mbps half-duplex (negotiation auto false, duplex half) while R1 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 is left to autonegotiate (negotiation auto true). Autonegotiation on one side with forced settings on the other commonly produces a duplex mismatch (one side full, the other half), which explains the elevated input_errors observed on both interfaces. Remediation is required to align speed/duplex settings.

messages.content:
What was observed
- Both ends of the R1↔R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 link are up but showing elevated input_errors (R1: 127, R2: 134). R1 is set to autonegotiate; R2 is forced to 100 Mbps half‑duplex.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- Duplex/speed configuration mismatch: R2 is manually configured for 100Mbps half‑duplex while R1 is autonegotiating. This mismatch is the most likely cause of the input_errors.

Changes attempted
- Attempted to ensure R2 Gi0/0/1 was up (set_interface_state called); interface was already enabled. No change was made to negotiation/duplex/speed.

Verification
- Result: FAILED.
  - R2 running-config still shows negotiation auto: false, duplex: half, speed: 100 Mbps.
  - Interface counters still show input_errors on both R1 (127) and R2 (134).
  - No evidence the planned change (enable autonegotiation on R2) was applied.

What remains / next recommended step
- Apply autonegotiation on R2 GigabitEthernet0/0/1 (or otherwise align both sides to the same speed/duplex). Expect a brief traffic interruption when changing negotiation. After applying, re-check interface counters to confirm input_errors clear.
